III.

Professional Impact

Jul 2017 – PresentBogotá, Colombia

Banco Davivienda S.A.

Corporate Credit — Eight Years, Four Roles
  • Corporate Credit, Services & Industry (Dec 2023 – Present): Leads financial analysis and evaluation of highly complex corporate and commercial credit transactions, specializing in trust accounts, fiduciary arrangements, joint ventures, and consortia. Formulates and defends strategic recommendations before executive credit committees, and directs special projects for the Corporate Credit Vice-Presidency.
  • Infrastructure & Projects Credit (Feb 2021 – Dec 2023): Structured financial and risk analyses for infrastructure-sector companies and macro-projects across MSME, SME, Commercial, and Corporate segments. Presented and defended complex financial models across bank departments and vice presidencies.
  • International Subsidiaries Operations (Dec 2018 – Feb 2021): Managed operational requests, re-documentation, and capital transfers for international subsidiary clients, with strategic advisory to the commercial sales force.
  • Internship, National Government Banking (Jul 2017 – Dec 2018): Supported financial modeling and solution design for corporate clients, with research on government reforms affecting commercial development.
Selected Transactions
Hotel Zuana Internal Grupo Bolívar credit facility, COP $60,000 million — through development
Grupo Ajover Loan refinancing requiring a revision of the project's core structure
Grupo Jamar Loan refinancing through a difficult negotiation with the investment bank
Infrastructure Portfolio Additional facilities for solar farms, roadways, and power substations
Jun 2021 – Apr 2026Bogotá, Colombia

Universidad Externado de Colombia

Master in Economics — Thesis Research
  • Thesis: "La relación no lineal entre la inclusión financiera y la estabilidad financiera" — an empirical study across 17 Latin American countries (2000–2020), examining how expanding access to financial services interacts with banking-system stability.
  • Built a Financial Stability Index via Principal Component Analysis and applied fixed-effects panel models, quantile regressions, and semiparametric estimation to test for non-linear effects.
  • Found that the relationship follows a "U" shape: financial inclusion initially weakens stability through higher credit risk and information asymmetries, with stabilizing effects only emerging after a critical threshold — a threshold most of the region has yet to reach.
  • Director: Jacobo Campo Robledo, Faculty of Economics.

IV.

Purpose

The purpose is about doing great things and creating value for society. This purpose drives the need for better financial and fiduciary structures for the development of these great ideas.

Figures Who Shape How I Think

Warren Buffett

Has shown that rational thinking, patience, and integrity are enduring competitive advantages. His disciplined approach to investing is a reminder that sustainable success is built through careful analysis, long-term vision, and the humility to keep learning.

Steve Jobs

Demonstrated that innovation emerges at the intersection of technology, creativity, and simplicity. His relentless pursuit of excellence is an invitation to challenge conventional thinking and build solutions that are technically sound and genuinely meaningful.

Nelson Mandela

Embodied resilience, forgiveness, and principled leadership. His life is a reminder that true leadership is measured not by authority, but by the ability to unite people around a shared purpose, even in the face of extraordinary adversity.

Sir Alex Ferguson

Built one of the most successful sporting organizations in history by developing people, maintaining discipline, and adapting continuously over decades — proof that sustained excellence depends on culture, accountability, and the willingness to evolve while staying faithful to core values.
